channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
item.description: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.
item.content:encoded: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

item.title: How long will the war last? No one knows, and it's making oil prices weird
item.description: It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.
item.content:encoded:
It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Americans seek redemption at figure skating worlds, just weeks after the Olympics
item.description: Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.
item.content:encoded:
Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.

(Image credit: Michal Cizek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
item.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops
item.description: A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.

(Image credit: Fatemeh Bahrami)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
item.description: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.
item.content:encoded: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

item.title: Rubio meets with other G7 nations for first time since Iran war
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio meets his G7 colleagues in Paris for the first time since the start of the war on Iran.
item.content:encoded: Secretary of State Rubio meets his G7 colleagues in Paris for the first time since the start of the war on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Southeast Asia is being hit hard by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas
item.description: Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.
item.content:encoded:
Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.

(Image credit: Aaron Favila)
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
item.description: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.
item.content:encoded: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
item.description: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy
